rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the component type scope when contracts relate to component ports
Use implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings after subcomponents and connections
Preserve existing AADL architecture by inserting annexes in valid scopes without modifying other elements
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks in the most specific valid scope that contains all referenced identifiers
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless they are represented through variables or outputs
Use named constants or visible relations for concrete bounds in assumptions and guarantees
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE expressions unless they are exposed as typed ports or constants
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `eq` for equations, `const` for constants, `assign` for implementation-level bindings
Comparison expressions are of type bool, integer arithmetic is of type int, real arithmetic is of type real
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees in scopes where referenced identifiers are not visible
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions without proper exposure
irrelevant_topics: AGREE annex placement for assumptions and guarantees
